title: /v3.0.0/libpod/networks/create ignores name
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Using the API call with `v3.0.0` for `/libpod/networks/create` ignores the `name` parameter, whereas a slightly different API call works.

```
# curl -H "Content-Type: application/json" --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock 'http://d/v3.0.0/libpod/networks/create' -d '{"name": "doesnotwork"}'
{"Filename":"/etc/cni/net.d/cni-podman1.conflist"}
# curl -H "Content-Type: application/json" --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock 'http://d/v3.0.0/libpod/networks/create' -d '{"name": "doesnotwork"}'
{"Filename":"/etc/cni/net.d/cni-podman2.conflist"}
# curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock 'libpod/networks/create' -d '{"name": "works"}'
{"Id":"93f6e11d9ce3e407e0eef9c87aacabf3f31235a92403845bb5093b4c6f01f2e5","Warning":null}
# curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock 'libpod/networks/create' -d '{"name": "works"}'
{"cause":"the network name works is already used","message":"the network name works is already used","response":500}
# podman network ls
NETWORK ID    NAME         VERSION     PLUGINS
2f259bab93aa  podman       0.4.0       bridge,portmap,firewall,tuning
b932778640d3  cni-podman1  0.4.0       bridge,portmap,firewall,tuning
603e960751dc  cni-podman2  0.4.0       bridge,portmap,firewall,tuning
93f6e11d9ce3  works        0.4.0       bridge,portmap,firewall,tuning
```

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Use the `curl` examples at the top of the API documentation to send a request to `http://d/v3.0.0/libpod/networks/create` using a body with some name.

2. See that the network is created as if no name had been provided.

3. Send a similar request to `libpod/networks/create` and see that the passed name is used for the network

**Describe the results you received:**

Calls to the documented `libpod/networks/create` endpoint resulted in networks of the form `cni-podman1`.

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expected both calls to use the `name` parameter provided




**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
# podman version
Version:      3.4.1-dev
API Version:  3.4.1-dev
Go Version:   go1.16.7
Built:        Tue Oct 19 09:11:42 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

physical host running CentOS Stream 8

comments.body: This is the syntax for the new 4.0 version. For version 3 and older you have to use `/v.3.0.0/libpod/networks/create?name=myname`
